goal: Find one or more new orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work where active-signal-v1@1 is absent, prove their additive value, and avoid repeating v1's single-filter event-entry mistakes.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable: no retuning of hours, days, thresholds, or execution rules.
Raw single-filter event-entry constructions (H1/H2 style) are permanently banned.
Every new candidate must first pass complement-first mode before any other evaluation.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction Wave A is fully exhausted.
Each prototype must have at least three logical layers: new information layer, regime/state confirmation, and entry-density limiter.
A candidate whose profit vanishes under strict-additive mode is rejected regardless of permissive-integrated performance.

slices.title: Feature contract and custom feature publication for Wave A
slices.hypothesis: Translate hypotheses into causally correct features with explicit source, lookback, warm-up behavior, and human-readable interpretation.
slices.objective: Translate hypotheses into causally correct features with explicit source, lookback, warm-up behavior, and human-readable interpretation.
success_criteria: All Wave A features are published, causality-checked, materialized, and verified.
evidence_requirements: All Wave A features are published, causality-checked, materialized, and verified.

slices.title: Hypothesis plausibility on base-absent surface
slices.hypothesis: Perform early triage of hypothesis prototypes exclusively on the complement surface where the base is absent, rejecting broad or noisy candidates before building full signals.
slices.objective: Perform early triage of hypothesis prototypes exclusively on the complement surface where the base is absent, rejecting broad or noisy candidates before building full signals.
success_criteria: Only plausibility-approved prototypes remain, each showing structured effect specifically where the base is absent.
evidence_requirements: Only plausibility-approved prototypes remain, each showing structured effect specifically where the base is absent.

slices.title: Build strict specialist prototypes
slices.hypothesis: Convert surviving ideas into real signal prototypes with at least three logical layers, avoiding the weak single-filter architecture that doomed H1/H2.
slices.objective: Convert surviving ideas into real signal prototypes with at least three logical layers, avoiding the weak single-filter architecture that doomed H1/H2.
success_criteria: A small set of strict specialist prototypes exists, not a loose grid of weak filters.
evidence_requirements: A small set of strict specialist prototypes exists, not a loose grid of weak filters.
